title: rbac — developer configuration
description: RBAC's canonical config surface. Read alongside `config/permission.php` (spatie's shipped file) — our config OVERLAYS spatie's with Stackra-specific keys. Any key that exists in both, `rbac.*` wins on the code path (it's the tenant-configurable surface); spatie's key remains the substrate default for the vendor package's own paths.

cache.description: Permission cache configuration. See `caches.json` for the key patterns.

keys.description: Cache store name (must resolve via config('cache.stores')). Falls back to `array` in tests. `array` in production is a startup-fail via CacheStoreProbe.
Per-principal ResolvedPermissionSet TTL. Framework floor: 60s (below this the resolver stampedes DB). Framework ceiling: 3600s (above this cross-node propagation lags too long).
Cache key prefix. Overridable per-service so multiple services sharing a Redis instance don't cross-invalidate.
When true, auth's LoginAction fires PermissionCacheManager::warm($user) so the FIRST post-login request doesn't cold-resolve. Off = lazy warm on first check.
Broadcast cache invalidation on Redis pubsub for cross-node propagation. Off = each node's cache lags by up to rbac.cache.ttl_seconds. Required in multi-node deployments.
When true, TenantProvisioned event triggers TenantDefaultRoleSeeder for the tenant's business_type. Off = tenants start with zero roles and admins must manually seed.
`per_entitlement` = check `custom_roles` entitlement (Medium+); `always` = ignore entitlements (dev only); `never` = system roles only, no custom creation.
Hard cap on permission attach per role. Guards against pathological configurations where an admin attaches every permission to a single role.
Hard cap on active role assignments per principal. Above this, the assignment observer refuses with `PrincipalTooManyRoles` (422).
When true, every new permission must end with one of the allowed_suffixes. Enforced by `valid_permission_name` rule. Off = free-form permission names (dev only).
The scope-suffix set enforced by valid_permission_name. See `auth::ScopeSuffixResolver` for how each suffix translates to a query filter.
Permission names starting with any of these are refused as custom permissions (owned by the platform, not by tenants).
Enforced by valid_permission_name. Spatie's `name` column is VARCHAR(128) so this is a hard limit.
Master toggle for expires_at on model_has_roles. Off = ignore expires_at values from callers; assignments live forever until revoked.
Hard cap on the max distance from now() to expires_at at assignment time. Guards against 'permanent' assignments accidentally set via a distant timestamp.
How many days before expires_at to dispatch RoleAssignmentExpiringNotification. 0 = never notify.
Path to the JSON manifest driving `rbac:sync-role-definitions`. Absolute paths OK; relative paths resolved against config('app.base_path').
`strict` = fail seed on any missing referenced permission (must exist in the catalogue first). `permissive` = skip missing permissions with a warning.
When true, TenantDefaultRoleSeeder reports intended writes to logs without persisting. Useful during migrations.
When true (default), step 2 of the resolver algorithm evaluates access/grants. Off = pure RBAC without the grants overlay. Only for dev + tests — production must have grants on.
Whether step 4 (delegation) fires. Off = principals never inherit delegated permissions.

When the requested permission doesn't exist in the catalogue: `deny` = silent deny; `warn_and_deny` = deny + write a warn-severity log entry. `warn_and_deny` catches typos in `#[RequirePermission('users.viewaany')]`.
Fraction of permission checks that fire the `Permission Check Performed` analytics event. 0.001 = 0.1%. Sampling is critical because volume would be crushing (every gated request = one check).
Every mutating Artisan command (rbac:assign-role, rbac:revoke-role, rbac:seed-defaults --force) requires --reason=<text>. Off in dev; on in staging + prod.
